subtopic: Dutch East India Company (VOC) in India
title: Dutch East India Company (VOC) in India
focus: Combines the MoW archive details of the VOC with a geographical map of Dutch settlements in India.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 021]
The Archives of the Dutch East India Company (Successfully inscribed in 2013) (Joint Nomination)
- Jointly nominated by Netherlands, India, Indonesia, South Africa, and Sri Lanka, this collection documents trade, treaties, and governance from 1602-1795 CE.
- India's segment, located in Tamil Nadu, provides insights into colonial commerce and politics.
- The Dutch East India Company (VOC, Verenigde Oostindische Compagnie), founded in 1602 and liquidated in 1795, was the largest and most impressive of the early modern European trading companies operating in Asia.
- The VOC archives make up the most complete and extensive source on early modern world history anywhere with data relevant to the history of hundreds of Asia's and Africa's former local political and trade regions.

[Page 022]
NETHERLANDS/DUTCH
Map: Dutch Settlements in India
Settlements and associated years:
- Chinsura: (1625), (1653)
- Masulipatnam: (1598), (1616)
- Pulicat: (1600)
- Nagapatam: (1507), (1658)
- Jaffna: (1658)
- Trincomale: (1522), (1639), (1674), (1673)
- Matara: (1507), (1656)
- Galle: (1507), (1656)
- Colombo: (1505), (1656)
- Cochin: (1500), (1669)
Geographical Markers:
- Arabian Sea
- Bay of Bengal
